body_txt: In the world of professional mascots, silence is golden and gestures are your best friend. There's a general rule that mascots are nonverbal happy-go-lucky goofballs and are often escorted around by their handlers note Although in very rare cases, there are some costumed mascots that are encouraged to talk . After all, a talking giant duck or foam-headed wolf could ruin the magic for the kiddos (and open up a lot of awkward questions). But sometimes, the person inside just can't help themselves. Maybe they want to give directions. Maybe they're venting to a coworker. Maybe they're screaming because they're being chased by bees. Whatever the case, the moment they open their mouth, the illusion dies — and hilarity (or horror) ensues. This trope shows up in comedies, mockumentaries, behind-the-scenes drama, and even horror. It's often Played for Laughs — especially if the voice is comically incongruous with the costume — but can also be used to show the human behind the mask, especially when the character inside is less cheerful than their exterior suggests. If it's Played for Horror , it may be a Creepy Mascot Suit . For the trope to take effect, the mascot costume in question must not have a hole for the performer's whole face to show through, however a screened hole or a hole that obscures the character's mouth is permissible. Also, the mascot cannot be a living anthropomorphic being. If the mascot in question is intended to speak, then it doesn't count. To avoid this page being overcrowded with real-life mascot bloopers, No Real Life Examples, Please! Overlaps with Loser Team Mascot . Compare Suddenly Speaking , with which this could overlap.
